name: basicOpen_eq_of_affine
nl_statement: For a commutative ring R and element f ∈ R, the basic open set in the spectrum scheme Spec(R) corresponding to f equals the basic open set in the prime spectrum of R.
nl_assumptions: R is a commutative ring
f is an element of R
nl_steps: Reduce to showing membership equivalence: x ∈ basicOpen(f) in scheme iff x ∈ basicOpen(f) in prime spectrum
Characterize membership via unit status in the stalk: x ∈ basicOpen(f) iff the germ of f at x is a unit
Use the isomorphism between stalk and fiber ring to relate units in the localized fiber ring
Apply the localization criterion at prime ideals: an element is a unit in the localization at a prime iff it is not in the prime ideal

name: germ_eq_zero_of_pow_mul_eq_zero
nl_statement: If s^n · f = 0 as global sections on an open U of a scheme X, and x is a point in the basic open of s, then the germ of f at x is zero.
nl_assumptions: X is a scheme
U is an open subspace of X
x is a point in U
s and f are global sections on U
s^n · f = 0 in Γ(X, U)
x is in the basic open set of s (i.e., s is nonzero at x)
nl_steps: Interpret the assumption 'x in basicOpen(s)' as the stalk germ of s^n being a unit in the stalk at x
Take the n-th power of the unit status of the germ of s
Use the fact that s^n is a unit to multiply both sides of s^n · f = 0 by its inverse
Map the resulting equation to the stalk to conclude the germ of f equals zero

name: Scheme.hom_inv_apply
nl_statement: For a scheme isomorphism e: X ≅ Y and a point x ∈ X, applying the inverse morphism to e(x) recovers x.
nl_assumptions: e is an isomorphism of schemes X and Y
x is a point in X
nl_steps: Recognize that e.inv ∘ e.hom equals the identity morphism on X
Apply this composition to the point x

name: Scheme.inv_hom_apply
nl_statement: For a scheme isomorphism e: X ≅ Y and a point y ∈ Y, applying the forward morphism to e.inv(y) recovers y.
nl_assumptions: e is an isomorphism of schemes X and Y
y is a point in Y
nl_steps: Recognize that e.hom ∘ e.inv equals the identity morphism on Y
Apply this composition to the point y

name: Spec_zeroLocus_eq_zeroLocus
nl_statement: For a commutative ring R and a subset s ⊆ R, the zero locus in Spec(R) of the image of s under the global sections isomorphism equals the zero locus of s in the prime spectrum.
nl_assumptions: R is a commutative ring
s is a subset of R
nl_steps: Reduce to proving equivalence of membership conditions at each prime ideal x
Translate: x is in the zero locus iff for all elements of s, their germs vanish at x
Use the fact that the germ vanishes iff the element is in the corresponding prime ideal

name: instance_IsLocalHom
nl_statement: The stalk map of any scheme morphism f at a point x is a local ring homomorphism.
nl_assumptions: f is a scheme morphism
x is a point in the domain of f
nl_steps: The stalk map is local by definition of scheme morphisms, which require locally ringed space morphisms

name: stalkMap_comp
nl_statement: For composable scheme morphisms f: X → Y and g: Y → Z, the stalk map of the composition equals the composition of the individual stalk maps at corresponding points.
nl_assumptions: f and g are composable scheme morphisms
nl_steps: Reduce to the presheafed space version of composition
Apply the presheafed space composition law for stalk maps

name: stalkSpecializes_stalkMap_apply
nl_statement: If x specializes to x' in X under the scheme morphism f, then the stalk map respects the specialization map: applying the stalk map commutes with applying the specialization map on the target.
nl_assumptions: f is a scheme morphism
x and x' are points in X with x ⤳ x' (x specializes to x')
y is an element of the stalk at x'
nl_steps: Use the commutativity relation stalkSpecializes_stalkMap relating stalk maps and specialization
Apply function congruence to extract pointwise equality for the element y

name: stalkMap_congr
nl_statement: When two scheme morphisms f and g are equal and points x and x' are equal (with compatible paths), the stalk maps are conjugate by the corresponding stalk congruence isomorphisms.
nl_assumptions: f = g as scheme morphisms
x = x' as points in X
nl_steps: Reduce to the locally ringed space version of the statement
Apply the locally ringed space congruence law for stalk maps

name: stalkMap_congr_hom
nl_statement: When two scheme morphisms f and g are equal at a fixed point x, the stalk map of f is conjugate to the stalk map of g via stalk congruence.
nl_assumptions: f = g as scheme morphisms
x is a fixed point in the domain
nl_steps: Reduce to locally ringed space
Apply the morphism congruence law

name: stalkMap_congr_point
nl_statement: When a point in the domain changes from x to x', the stalk maps are conjugate by the stalk congruence isomorphism.
nl_assumptions: f is a fixed scheme morphism
x = x' as points in the domain
nl_steps: Reduce to the locally ringed space version
Apply point congruence for stalk maps

name: stalkMap_hom_inv
nl_statement: For a scheme isomorphism e: X ≅ Y and point y ∈ Y, the composition of the forward stalk map at e.inv(y) with the inverse stalk map at y equals the stalk congruence isomorphism.
nl_assumptions: e is a scheme isomorphism X ≅ Y
y is a point in Y
nl_steps: Reduce to the locally ringed space category
Apply the isomorphism stalk map composition law

name: stalkMap_hom_inv_apply
nl_statement: Pointwise application: For an isomorphism e and element z in the stalk at e.inv(y), applying the inverse stalk map to the forward stalk map of z recovers the stalk congruence applied to z.
nl_assumptions: e is a scheme isomorphism
y is a point in Y
z is an element of the stalk at y
nl_steps: Use function extensionality on the equality stalkMap_hom_inv
Extract the pointwise equality for the element z

name: stalkMap_inv_hom
nl_statement: For a scheme isomorphism e: X ≅ Y and point x ∈ X, the composition of the inverse stalk map at e(x) with the forward stalk map at x equals the stalk congruence isomorphism.
nl_assumptions: e is a scheme isomorphism X ≅ Y
x is a point in X
nl_steps: Reduce to the locally ringed space category
Apply the inverse-forward composition law for stalk maps

name: stalkMap_inv_hom_apply
nl_statement: Pointwise application: For an isomorphism e and element y in the stalk at e.hom(x), applying the forward stalk map to the inverse stalk map of y recovers the stalk congruence applied to y.
nl_assumptions: e is a scheme isomorphism
x is a point in X
y is an element of the stalk at e(x)
nl_steps: Use function extensionality on stalkMap_inv_hom
Extract pointwise equality for the element y

name: germ_stalkMap
nl_statement: For a scheme morphism f: X → Y, an open U ⊆ Y, and point x ∈ X with f(x) ∈ U, the germ map to the stalk of Y composed with the stalk map equals the morphism on sections composed with the germ.
nl_assumptions: f is a scheme morphism
U is an open subset of Y
x is a point in X with f(x) ∈ U
nl_steps: Reduce to the presheafed space version of the statement
Apply the presheafed space compatibility law relating germs and stalk maps
